label: SAR Visualizer
description: Interactive 3D SAR observation-geometry visualizer (ported from sar-visualizer). Left pane: parameter sliders. Right pane: 3D satellite/ground/beam scene. Bottom: TX/RX timing diagram. Sliders update the 3D scene live and write the same parameters into the kernel (H, look, La, Lr, PRF, Tp, B, fc, V) at flow run time.
code_template:
# --- Library helpers (formerly Common Parameters node). Provided here so
# this sar_visualizer node can replace it: disable the Common Parameters node
# and these + the parameter assignments above fully set up the kernel. ---
def make_rand(seed):
    """mulberry32 PRNG (exact port). float in [0,1)."""
    box = {'a': int(seed) & 0xFFFFFFFF}
    M = 0xFFFFFFFF
    def _rand():
        box['a'] = (box['a'] + 0x6d2b79f5) & M
        t = box['a']
        t = (((t ^ (t >> 15)) & M) * ((1 | t) & M)) & M           # Math.imul(t^(t>>>15), 1|t)
        t = ((t + ((((t ^ (t >> 7)) & M) * ((61 | t) & M)) & M)) & M) ^ t  # +Math.imul(t^(t>>>7),61|t), ^t
        t &= M
        return ((t ^ (t >> 14)) & M) / 4294967296.0
    return _rand

def make_point_targets(count, half_az_m, half_range_m, seed, R0):
    """Point targets, sar-visualizer parity. T1 is always the centre
    reference (az 0, slant R0); extras are seeded-random inside +/-0.85*half
    so they sit clearly within the display window."""
    out = [('T1', 0.0, R0)]
    rnd = make_rand(seed)
    az_span = half_az_m * 0.85
    r_span = half_range_m * 0.85
    for i in range(1, int(count)):
        az_m = (rnd() * 2 - 1) * az_span
        sr_m = R0 + (rnd() * 2 - 1) * r_span
        out.append(('T%d' % (i + 1), az_m, sr_m))
    return out
# Plot helpers (surface3d_gl, surface3d_pair_gl, upsample_complex, upsample_complex_range, ...) are
# defined in the "Plot Library" node (unconnected, runs first) and shared via
# the kernel namespace. They are workspace-local, NOT HiyoCanvas core. See that
# node's SPEC and CLAUDE.md ("common helpers go in a visible Library node").
